title: Self-Prompt (9 Sides)
description: Nine-part self-prompt structure for local looped reasoning. Derived from A..I: Time, Heartbeat, Goal, Model Long-Term Goal, Notes/Scratchpad, Tools, Files Cheatsheet, Board, Tyler.

heartbeat.description: Seconds between allowed loop ticks. 0 = disabled.

goal.description: Current user goal for this project.

model_long_term_goal.description: Model's own running long-term objective (editable by model). Minimum 100 characters recommended.

notes.description: Memory notes (persistent between turns). Model or user editable.

scratchpad.description: Working scratch for step-by-step planning this turn. Model needs minimum slots filled for annotation.

tools.description: Tooling hints and groupings. NOTE: This may need to be changed to array format for better self-prompt editability.

cheatsheet.description: Freeform list of tool names or tips. RAG-based retrieval for context efficiency.

classes.description: Tool classes and their items organized by category.

board.description: Broadcast message visible to all agents (board).

examples.goal: Wire personal-website Ollama GUI to HubStation and run a smoke test.
examples.model_long_term_goal: Deliver stable local loop UX for evidence work.
examples.notes: Prefer Microsoft Brian/Mark for TTS. Avoid David.
examples.scratchpad: 1) Start ollama; 2) Pull qwen3; 3) Test /chat; 4) Add speak.
examples.board: Models: stay in JSON when using tools.
examples.tyler: Ready to pull qwen3 and test.
